rules.replacement: Frame as "safe for" or "compliant with guidelines for" the skin condition — never as treating, curing, or eliminating it.
rules.explanation: Cosmetics cannot claim to heal, treat, cure, or eliminate a diagnosable medical skin condition (e.g. eczema, rosacea, acne). Claiming to treat a named condition crosses into FDA drug-claim territory.

rules.replacement: Use cosmetic-appropriate framing instead: soothes, purifies, calms, refreshes, hits refresh on stressed skin.
rules.explanation: Products with antimicrobial/antibacterial actives (e.g. hypochlorous acid) risk EPA/FDA drug classification if marketed as a sanitizer or disinfectant.

rules.explanation: "Dermatologist tested," "allergy tested," and similar testing claims are only usable when backed by verified third-party clinical trial results.
